https://github.com/Eugeny/tabby.reason: Electron GUI app; no meaningful headless run

https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty.reason: GUI OpenGL terminal; no meaningful headless run

https://github.com/aseprite/aseprite.reason: Aseprite is a GUI pixel art editor that cannot run without a display on Linux. The laf GUI framework (used by aseprite) unconditionally initialises X11 in main() before app_main() is called — XOpenDisplay(nullptr) returns NULL without $DISPLAY set, and XOpenIM(NULL,...) crashes immediately. This happens even in --batch mode. While the binary compiles with X11 dev headers, there is no headless runtime path and no honest smoke is possible. The default Skia rendering backend also requires prebuilt Skia binaries not available as Ubuntu packages. LAF_BACKEND=none avoids Skia but the X11 startup crash remains. Same category as alacritty, imhex, and tabby.

https://github.com/chubin/cheat.sh.reason: server + data-fetch heavy; only one trivial offline unit test, no honest offline smoke

https://github.com/coder/code-server.reason: vendors all of VS Code (multi-GB, long build); out of scope

https://github.com/commaai/openpilot.reason: openpilot's pyproject.toml defines no scripts or entry-points, so there is no CLI to smoke-test. The build requires 22+ vendored C/C++ native libraries (FFmpeg, Eigen, ZeroMQ, libjpeg, compiler toolchains) sourced from a custom GitHub repository plus pycapnp (needs compiled capnproto headers) — effectively a multi-GB native build chain. Normal operation manages ADAS subprocesses interfacing with a car's CAN bus via hardware; tests require replay data or hardware-in-the-loop. No cached toolchain covers this build and no honest headless smoke exists.

https://github.com/electron/electron.reason: Building Electron from source requires depot_tools (gn/ninja) and gclient to sync the full ~20 GB Chromium source tree, then a multi-hour compilation — far beyond c_rootfs (autotools/cmake/g++ only). A new combined toolchain bundling gn, ninja, and the Chromium build infrastructure would be required. Even after a successful build the binary is a GUI browser runtime with no meaningful headless smoke. Same category as zen-browser (Mozilla build system) and code-server (multi-GB vendor build).

https://github.com/genymobile/scrcpy.reason: scrcpy is split into a Java server APK (built with Gradle + Android SDK, deployed via adb) and a C client (meson + SDL2 + FFmpeg). The Android SDK is not in any cached toolchain, and the client requires SDL2 (display) and FFmpeg/libav dev packages not in c_rootfs. At runtime the app opens a GUI window showing the device screen — there is no headless or CLI mode and no meaningful smoke test without a connected Android device.

https://github.com/gyulyvgc/sniffnet.reason: sniffnet is a GUI desktop application built on iced/wgpu. Compiling it on Linux requires system libraries not present in rust_rootfs: libpcap-dev (for the pcap packet-capture crate), libgtk-3-dev (for the rfd native file-dialog crate), and X11/Wayland dev headers (for winit/iced). Even if compiled, the binary opens a graphical window for all normal operation with no meaningful headless mode. This places it in the same deferral category as alacritty, pake, and tabby.

https://github.com/janhq/jan.reason: could not land: verification failed {"build": true, "smoke": false, "tests": {}, "smoke_target_exists": true}

https://github.com/kovidgoyal/kitty.reason: kitty is a GPU-accelerated terminal emulator whose C extension (fast_data_types.so) links against libGL.so, libX11.so, and associated X11 libs. kitty/main.py does top-level imports that load this extension before any argument parsing, so even kitty --version would fail at dynamic-linker startup in python_rootfs (libGL/libX11 absent). Building requires an extensive apt set (libgl-dev, libx11-dev, libxrandr-dev, libxi-dev, libfontconfig-dev, GLFW C sources). Bundling the full GL+X11+GLFW runtime stack (far more transitive libs than manim's Cairo bundle) would technically enable kitty --version but yields no meaningful headless smoke of a terminal emulator. Same category as alacritty (OpenGL terminal, already deferred).

https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-.reason: needs a browser toolchain (Chrome/chromium ~646M); deferred until we invest in a cached playwright_rootfs

https://github.com/mifi/lossless-cut.reason: lossless-cut is an Electron + React GUI video editing application. It uses electron-vite with a renderer targeting chrome148, requires electron-builder install-app-deps (downloads native Electron binaries), and has no headless or CLI mode — the packaged app opens a desktop window for all operations. This is the same category as tabby (Electron, deferred) and pake (Tauri, deferred): a GUI-only desktop app with no meaningful headless execution path.

https://github.com/mudler/localai.reason: could not land: verification failed {"build": true, "smoke": false, "tests": {}, "smoke_target_exists": true}

https://github.com/obsproject/obs-studio.reason: OBS Studio is a Qt6 GUI desktop application for live streaming and screen recording. Its CMake build requires Qt6 dev packages, FFmpeg/libav headers, X11 or Wayland display headers, and many audio/video capture libraries (PulseAudio, V4L2, libx264, etc.) — none present in c_rootfs. The application initializes Qt QApplication before any argument processing, requiring a live display connection even for --version; there is no headless or CLI operation mode. A dedicated toolchain baking Qt6 + FFmpeg + display stack would be needed — the same investment flagged for qbittorrent. Deferred.

https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF.reason: needs an OCR toolchain (tesseract/ghostscript/unpaper/qpdf) + large real-OCR suite

https://github.com/ollama/ollama.reason: Ollama requires a two-phase build: cmake for the llama-server C++ backend and CGo compilation for x/imagegen and x/mlxrunner (both have #cgo directives with generated C headers produced by go generate). No cached toolchain covers both Go and cmake/g++. Building with CGO_ENABLED=0 fails because runner-> x/imagegen/mlx and x/mlxrunner/mlx are unconditionally imported with CGo. A new go_c_rootfs toolchain combining Go + cmake + build-essential is required; that decision is out of scope for this run.

https://github.com/pixijs/pixijs.reason: PixiJS is a browser WebGL/Canvas 2D rendering library with no CLI and no Node.js/headless mode. Its Jest test runner is @pixi/jest-electron, which boots a full Electron process to provide WebGL + Canvas 2D APIs — incompatible with node_rootfs. The built output targets browsers and fails immediately when imported in Node.js due to missing window/document/WebGLRenderingContext. There is no meaningful headless smoke: the library requires a live browser context for any operation beyond reading package.json metadata. Landing would require a playwright_rootfs or electron_rootfs toolchain baking Chromium alongside Node.js.

https://github.com/protocolbuffers/proto.reason: could not land: claude rc=1, no changes

https://github.com/qbittorrent/qbittorre.reason: qBittorrent's headless variant (qbittorrent-nox) avoids a display server but still links Qt6Core at runtime, which transitively requires the ~36 MB libicudata.so.74 ICU data library plus libQt6Network, libQt6Sql, libQt6Xml, and libtorrent-rasterbar. These are not in c_rootfs. Bundling them at build time would create an ~80-100 MB artifact of shared libs; running apt-get install in the smoke step risks the 300s sh_test timeout. Ubuntu Qt6 packages do not ship static libs, so static linking is unavailable without building Qt6 from source (itself a multi-hour build requiring a new toolchain). A dedicated c_qt_rootfs toolchain baking Qt6 + libtorrent-rasterbar is needed — toolchain investment is a human decision.

https://github.com/siddharthvaddem/opens.reason: openscreen is a GUI desktop application for screen recording and demo creation, the TypeScript equivalent of Screen Studio. Its repo contains CMakeLists.txt (cross-platform native screen-capture code), Package.swift (macOS AVFoundation/ScreenCaptureKit Swift wrapper), and package.json (Electron frontend) — the standard shape of an Electron app with native addons. The application opens a graphical window for all normal operation; there is no CLI or headless mode. Same deferral category as lossless-cut, tabby, pake, and alacritty.

https://github.com/spacedriveapp/spacedr.reason: Spacedrive is a Tauri v1 GUI desktop application (cross-platform file explorer). Building the desktop app on Linux requires libwebkit2gtk-4.0/4.1-dev and libgtk-3-dev which are not in rust_rootfs. The frontend is React/TypeScript requiring Node.js/pnpm alongside Rust, so neither rust_rootfs nor node_rootfs alone covers the full build. There is no standalone headless CLI or server mode that would provide an honest smoke test — the application's entire purpose is a graphical file explorer window. Same category as pake (Tauri v2) and alacritty.

https://github.com/tw93/pake.reason: Pake is a Tauri v2 desktop GUI application. Its src-tauri/Cargo.toml depends on tauri 2.10.2 and tauri-build 2.5.5. On Linux, Tauri v2 requires libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev, libgtk-3-dev, and related GTK/display-server headers for compilation — none of which are present in the rust_rootfs toolchain. The resulting app creates an OS window to render the wrapped URL; there is no meaningful headless execution path. This is the same deferral category as alacritty (OpenGL terminal) and tabby (Electron).

https://github.com/werwolv/imhex.reason: ImHex is a Dear ImGui graphical hex editor that initialises a GLFW/OpenGL context before processing any arguments — there is no --version early-exit or headless mode. Building requires libgl-dev, libglfw3-dev, libglew-dev, and dozens of CMake FetchContent dependencies. Running without a display fails immediately at OpenGL context creation. Whole-project deferral; same category as alacritty and sniffnet.

https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop.reason: zen-browser is a full Firefox-fork desktop browser. Building it requires the Mozilla build system (mach), clang, Rust, Python, and many native C/C++ libraries not present in any cached toolchain — multi-GB download and multi-hour build. The packaged application opens a GUI window for all operation; there is no --version early exit or headless mode without a display server. No meaningful smoke is possible. Deferred as a browser-toolchain investment decision, same as playwright-mcp.
